description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly dark gray coloration. The surface is coarse and uneven, featuring a mix of fine and larger speckled inclusions, likely feldspar or quartz, giving it a mottled appearance. One side of the rock displays a lighter patch, possibly due to weathering or mineral variation, while the rest of the surfaces are darker and more uniform. The edges are jagged and fractured, suggesting it was broken from a larger mass rather than smoothed by water or human intervention. The rock's mass and density are consistent with igneous or metamorphic stone, making it a substantial obstacle in an urban environment. Its size and shape would make it suitable as a natural barrier or landscaping element in parks, sidewalks, or roadside settings.
description: A rugged, dark gray rock with jagged edges and a speckled, coarse surface, featuring a lighter patch on one side. Its irregular form and substantial mass make it a prominent natural obstacle or landscaping element.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's angular top and a prominent lighter patch on the lower right side, contrasting with the otherwise dark, speckled surface.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock appears more compact and the surface is almost entirely dark, with rough, uneven texture and minimal visible inclusions.
description_view_2: This side shows a broad, flat face with a consistent dark gray color and fine speckling, as well as a small notch at the bottom edge.
description_view_3: Here, the rock's jagged, pointed end is visible, with a rough, dark surface and subtle variations in texture and color.
